title: Defeat the Dark Side

'''''Defeat the Dark Side''''' was a unified advertising campaign for three restaurants owned by [[Wikipedia:Yum!_Brands|Tricon Global]]—[[Wikipedia:KFC|KFC]], [[Wikipedia:Pizza Hut|Pizza Hut]], and [[Wikipedia:Taco Bell|Taco Bell]]—that ran prior to and concurrent with the theatrical release of the [[1999]] [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I: ''The Phantom Menace'']]. It depicted mascots from all three franchises involved in the events surrounding the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]. The campaign was reportedly viewed as unsuccessful by Tricon Global and [[Lucasfilm]], and they did not partner for the subsequent prequel films.

==Plot summary==
In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]] breaks out on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> KFC, Pizza Hut, and Taco Bell join forces to aid in the fight against the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]].<ref name="The Saga" />

==Development==
The ''Defeat the Dark Side'' campaign included seven television spots,<ref name="The Saga" /> toys and cup toppers available at the restaurants, a website,<ref name="Site Map">{{WebCite|url=http://defeatthedarkside.com/andwin/site_directory/default.htm|text=Site Map|archivedate=20000417143237|nolive=1}}</ref> and a limited-time sweepstakes game.<ref name="Game Over">{{WebCite|url=http://defeatthedarkside.com/andwin/Car/Games/game.htm|text=Game|archivedate=19991010085553|nolive=1}}</ref> The sweepstakes game required visiting all three [[Wikipedia:Yum!_Brands|Tricon Global]] restaurants for a chance to win prizes such as a [[Wikipedia:Lincoln Navigator|Lincoln Navigator]] vehicle, one million dollars, or a ''Star Wars'' hovercraft speeder.<ref name="Promotional Onslaught Begins">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/episode1/story/taco_bellkfcpizza_hut_promotional_onslaught_begins_80517.asp|text=Taco Bell/KFC/Pizza Hut Promotional Onslaught Begins|date=1999-05-11|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20160413150152}}</ref> During the sweepstakes, the restaurants were aligned to different planets: [[Wikipedia:KFC|KFC]] with Naboo, [[Wikipedia:Pizza Hut|Pizza Hut]] with [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and [[Wikipedia:Taco Bell|Taco Bell]] with [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="Site Map" />

==Release and reception==
According to Tricon Global's chairman and chief executive, Andrall Pearson, the tie-in marketing campaign was not effective at driving sales to the three restaurant chains and appeared to have stalled momentum from prior quarters.<ref name="LA Times">{{WebCite|url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-1999-jul-21-fi-58041-story.html|text=Force of 'Star Wars' Tie Not Aiding Taco Bell|author=Hernandez, Greg|date=1999-07-21|work=Los Angeles Times|archiveurl=https://archive.is/aZzoG}}</ref> The failure was attributed to the market's supersaturation, with ''[[Star Wars]]'' merchandise available at many retailers in advance of the film.<ref name="WSJ">{{WebCite|url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/SB932508974496770985|text=Tricon's 'Star Wars' Tie-In Fails to Boost Fast-Food Sales|author=Gibson, Richard|date=1999-07-21|work=The Wall Street Journal|archiveurl=https://archive.is/fiHe5}}</ref> The campaign was reportedly considered a failure by [[Lucasfilm]] as well, and they did not partner with Tricon for the next prequel film.<ref name="Lucasfilm Splits with Tricon">{{WebCite|url=https://www.theforce.net/collecting/story/Lucasfilm_Splits_with_Tricon_71624.asp|text=Lucasfilm Splits with Tricon|date=2001-07-24|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20210327035204}}</ref>
